data.title: As the DSO workflow, I can accept an engineer's PR comment by applying the requested change, committing, and replying with the commit SHA
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Orchestrator logic for the accept action handler: dispatches a general-purpose fix sub-agent to implement the change requested in the comment, commits and pushes the result, and replies to the comment thread with the commit SHA.
**Why**: Accepting an engineer's change request is the highest-value response — it directly implements feedback and closes the comment loop without manual intervention.
**Scope**:
- IN: Receive a pre-classified accept comment (with normalized JSON including path/position fields); dispatch a general-purpose fix sub-agent with the comment body, the file identified by path (and nearby context), and the current PR diff as context; handle failure cases: if sub-agent signals multi-file scope, returns no changes, or reports a conflict — revert ALL partial working-tree changes (including staged changes, unstaged edits, and any new files) before reclassifying to defer per Story 4; handle non-fast-forward push (branch head moved) as a conflict → revert and reclassify to defer; on clean success, commit and push to PR branch, then use the shared reply-posting function from Story 1 to post a thread reply with the commit SHA; all reply bodies begin with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->
- OUT: Comment classification (Story 5); comment fetching and schema (Story 1); comments not classified as accept

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, an accept-classified comment causes a fix sub-agent to apply the requested change, and the handler commits and pushes the result to the PR branch
  ← Satisfies: "a general-purpose fix sub-agent receives the comment body, relevant source files, and current diff" (SC2)
- When this story is complete, if the fix sub-agent signals multi-file scope, returns no changes, or reports a conflict, ALL partial working-tree changes are completely reverted and the comment is reclassified to defer (no leftover staged or unstaged changes after revert)
  ← Satisfies: "orchestrator reverts any partial working-tree changes and reclassifies to defer per SC4" (SC2)
- When this story is complete, a non-fast-forward push failure (branch head moved) also triggers revert and reclassify to defer
  ← Satisfies: "non-fast-forward triggers reclassify-to-defer" (epic Scenario Analysis)
- When this story is complete, the thread reply includes the commit SHA of the applied fix and begins with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->
  ← Satisfies: "commits and pushes and posts a reply referencing the commit SHA; all reply bodies begin with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->" (SC2)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done requirement

## Considerations

- [Security] Comment body from engineer is passed to fix sub-agent as context — pass via file or safe argument quoting, never interpolated directly into shell commands (A03:2021 Injection)
- [Reliability] Revert of partial working-tree changes must succeed even when the sub-agent modified multiple files; test must confirm the working tree is fully clean (git status --porcelain = empty) after revert before reclassify proceeds
- [Reliability] Non-fast-forward push should be detected from the push exit code and stderr; treat identically to a conflict — full revert and reclassify to defer
- [Testing] Test must cover the partial-failure path: sub-agent modifies file A, then signals conflict; handler must revert all changes to A before reclassifying
- [Maintainability] Use the shared thread-reply posting function from Story 1 for the reply call — do not reimplement is_inline endpoint routing here

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
